title: SieveError::source is pub(crate), callers cannot downcast
description: SieveError::source field is pub(crate). The doc comment says callers can downcast via err.source().and_then(|e| e.downcast_ref::<ParseError>()). But std::error::Error::source() returns Option<&dyn Error + 'static>; downcast_ref requires Any which is not in scope for Error by default. More importantly: once callers take a dependency on the ParseError downcast pattern, making the source field public (or changing ParseError's structure) is a breaking change. The current pub(crate) visibility means the std::error::Error::source() chain works for display/chaining but not programmatic inspection. The doc comment in parse_error.rs advertises this downcast capability to external callers, creating an expectation that is not fully supported and will require a breaking change to stabilize.
